ddls:
CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the region */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the region is located */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the region */,
  "region_code" TEXT /* Unique code for the region */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the region */,
  "geographical_area" REAL /* Geographical area of the region in square kilometers */,
  "timezone" TEXT /* Timezone of the region */,
  "language" TEXT /* Primary language spoken in the region */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data source */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL for the data source */,
  "data_source_type" TEXT /* Type of data source (e.g., government, hospital, research institute) */,
  "reliability_score" INTEGER /* Reliability score of the data source */,
  "last_verified_date" TEXT /* Date when the data source was last verified */,
  "contact_person" TEXT /* Name of the primary contact person at the data source */,
  "contact_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the primary contact person */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_entries" (
  "entry_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data entry */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region where the data was collected */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data source */,
  "last_update" TEXT /* Date of the last update */,
  "confirmed_cases" INTEGER /* Number of confirmed cases */,
  "deaths" INTEGER /* Number of deaths */,
  "recovered_cases" INTEGER /* Number of recovered cases */,
  "data_type_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the type of data */,
  "data_collection_method" TEXT /* Method used to collect the data */,
  "data_accuracy" INTEGER /* Accuracy score of the data */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Any additional notes or comments about the data entry */,
  "reporting_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency at which the data is reported */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("entry_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_entries_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_entries_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_entries_data_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_type_id") REFERENCES data_types ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the data type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each historical data entry */,
  "entry_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data entry */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the historical entry */,
  "confirmed_cases" INTEGER /* Number of confirmed cases */,
  "deaths" INTEGER /* Number of deaths */,
  "recovered_cases" INTEGER /* Number of recovered cases */,
  "data_type_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the type of data */,
  "data_collection_method" TEXT /* Method used to collect the data */,
  "data_accuracy" INTEGER /* Accuracy score of the data */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Any additional notes or comments about the historical data entry */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_history_entry_id FOREIGN KEY ("entry_id") REFERENCES data_entries ("entry_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_history_data_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_type_id") REFERENCES data_types ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_files" (
  "file_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data file */,
  "entry_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data entry the file belongs to */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path to the data file */,
  "file_size" REAL /* Size of the data file in MB */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the file was uploaded */,
  "file_format" TEXT /* Format of the data file */,
  "file_checksum" TEXT /* Checksum to verify the integrity of the file */,
  "file_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the content of the file */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("file_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_files_entry_id FOREIGN KEY ("entry_id") REFERENCES data_entries ("entry_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "readme_files" (
  "readme_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each readme file */,
  "entry_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data entry the readme file belongs to */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path to the readme file */,
  "file_size" REAL /* Size of the readme file in MB */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the file was uploaded */,
  "file_format" TEXT /* Format of the readme file */,
  "file_checksum" TEXT /* Checksum to verify the integrity of the file */,
  "file_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the content of the readme file */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("readme_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_readme_files_entry_id FOREIGN KEY ("entry_id") REFERENCES data_entries ("entry_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., healthcare professional, researcher, admin) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department or organization the user belongs to */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Level of access the user has */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "entry_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data entry being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data entry */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data entry was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the data */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the user's browser or device */,
  "access_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the access event in seconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_entry_id FOREIGN KEY ("entry_id") REFERENCES data_entries ("entry_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "entry_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data entry the report is based on */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date the report was generated */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of the report (e.g., daily, weekly, monthly) */,
  "report_file_path" TEXT /* File path to the report */,
  "generated_by" TEXT /* User who generated the report */,
  "report_summary" TEXT /* Brief summary of the report's findings */,
  "report_status" TEXT /* Status of the report (e.g., draft, published, archived) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_entry_id FOREIGN KEY ("entry_id") REFERENCES data_entries ("entry_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user receiving the notification */,
  "entry_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data entry the notification is about */,
  "notification_date" TEXT /* Date the notification was sent */,
  "message" TEXT /* Message of the notification */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., email, SMS, in-app) */,
  "notification_status" TEXT /* Status of the notification (e.g., sent, failed, pending) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_entry_id FOREIGN KEY ("entry_id") REFERENCES data_entries ("entry_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_preferences" (
  "preference_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user preference */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "notification_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of notifications (e.g., daily, weekly) */,
  "data_access_type" TEXT /* Type of data access (e.g., real-time, batch) */,
  "preferred_language" TEXT /* Preferred language for notifications and data access */,
  "preferred_timezone" TEXT /* Preferred timezone for notifications and data access */,
  "preferred_units" TEXT /* Preferred units for data display (e.g., metric, imperial) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("preference_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_preferences_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
